title: Advancing Renewable Energy Policy Frameworks in Sub-Saharan Africa: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Dr. Amina Okonkwo, Prof. Kofi Asante, Dr. Fatima Al-Rashid
abstract: This study examines the evolution of renewable energy policies across twelve Sub-Saharan African nations, identifying key success factors and barriers to sustainable energy transition.

title: Digital Financial Inclusion and Economic Empowerment: Evidence from Mobile Money in Rural Kenya
authors: Dr. Grace Wanjiku, Prof. Samuel Oduya
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of mobile money services on household economic outcomes in rural Kenya. Using panel data from 2,500 households over five years.

title: Traditional Medicine Integration in Contemporary Healthcare Systems: A Nigerian Perspective
authors: Prof. Oluwaseun Adeyemi, Dr. Chika Nwosu, Dr. Blessing Okeke
abstract: This research explores pathways for integrating traditional medicine practices into Nigeria's formal healthcare system. Through mixed-methods research.

title: Constitutional Reforms and Democratic Consolidation in Post-Conflict African States
authors: Prof. Jean-Pierre Mukiza, Dr. Amara Sesay
abstract: This article analyzes constitutional reform processes in Rwanda, Liberia, and Côte d'Ivoire, examining how post-conflict legal frameworks can support sustainable democracy.

title: Machine Learning Applications in African Agricultural Systems: A Systematic Review
authors: Dr. Tendai Moyo, Prof. Aisha Mohammed, Dr. Peter Nkrumah
abstract: This systematic review synthesizes findings from 87 studies on machine learning applications in African agriculture. We identify successful implementations.

title: Youth Unemployment and Social Entrepreneurship: Innovations from Lagos
authors: Dr. Folake Adebayo, Prof. Emeka Okafor
abstract: This study examines how social entrepreneurship initiatives are addressing youth unemployment in Lagos, Nigeria. Through case studies of 15 successful ventures.

title: Climate Change Adaptation Strategies in Coastal West African Communities
authors: Dr. Kwame Asante, Prof. Ngozi Eze
abstract: An empirical study of climate adaptation strategies employed by coastal communities across Ghana, Benin, and Nigeria, examining indigenous knowledge integration.

title: Artificial Intelligence in African Healthcare: Opportunities and Ethical Challenges
authors: Prof. Amara Diallo, Dr. Ruth Kimani
abstract: This paper examines the deployment of AI diagnostic tools in resource-limited African healthcare settings, identifying key opportunities and ethical considerations.
